title: Trigonometry, Vectors, and Periodic Systems · Independent Model Trial
summary: Plan bearings, slopes, cyclic demand, and resultant forces. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a navigation and periodic-demand model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use radians and the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and periodicity.
Apply right-triangle ratios with diagrams, units, and angle conditions.
Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 3 units and 4 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(3² + 4²) and check that the result exceeds either component.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use √(3² + 4²) and check that the result exceeds either component.
